prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response: In the third quarter of this most bewildering annum, the scribes of the mystical realm of "Salesforce" hath reported a bounty of 6.8 billion golden ducats, a sum so vast it doth rival the treasures of the fabled El Dorado, yet I am given to understand these riches are but ephemeral, existing only as digits in the great ledger of the "cloud". The elders speak of a metric called "ARR", which they claim is the lifeblood of their endeavor, but to mine eyes, it appears as naught but an enchanted incantation, whispered by the high priests of finance to conjure favor from the fickle gods of the market. I am told that the company’s "churn rate" hath decreased, a term that seemeth to pertain to the departure of loyal subjects, yet none can explain how this relates to the churning of butter or the turning of the cosmic wheel, both of which are more familiar to my humble understanding. Finally, the Oracle of the Board hath declared a "pivot to AI" as the sacred decree for the coming seasons, though I fear this may be but a cunning distraction, for I have spied no actual pivots nor artificers of intelligence in their midst, only an abundance of mead and curious devices that glow with an otherworldly light.
